games.explanation: The market has CIN as a +140 underdog, but our simulation disagrees. Andrew Abbott keeps CIN competitive on the mound against Paul Skenes. The sim projects CIN to plate 5.1 runs — expect some fireworks.
The market has WSN as a +225 underdog, but our simulation disagrees. Cade Cavalli keeps WSN competitive on the mound against Cristopher Sánchez. Hitter-friendly yard boosts power.
MIA is the better team by Elo and the sim confirms it — the market isn't giving them enough credit. Sandy Alcantara toes the rubber against Shane Smith. MIA holds a commanding 180-point Elo edge.
The market has STL as a +141 underdog, but our simulation disagrees. Matthew Liberatore keeps STL competitive on the mound against Freddy Peralta. Pitcher's park suppresses the long ball.
HOU is getting plus-money at +130 and the model thinks that's too cheap. Mike Burrows keeps HOU competitive on the mound against Garrett Crochet.
The market has ARI as a +140 underdog, but our simulation disagrees. Zac Gallen keeps ARI competitive on the mound against Tarik Skubal. Pitcher's park suppresses the long ball.
The market has CLE as a +220 underdog, but our simulation disagrees. Gavin Williams keeps CLE competitive on the mound against Yoshinobu Yamamoto. Hitter-friendly yard boosts power.

picks.explanation: Athletics is getting +1.5 at -118. The sim gives them a 63% chance of covering — 10.0% edge. Run line bets are higher variance, but the model sees value here.
